talk_title: Granulocyte reprogramming in gastric metaplasia and phosphodiesterase inhibition as neoadjuvant therapy

biomarkers.name: SLFN12L-positive granulocytic MDSC
biomarkers.finding: frequency increased from normal mucosa to intestinal metaplasia to tumor; decreased after 2 weeks of tadalafil

biomarkers.name: ARG1 expression
biomarkers.finding: detectable arginase‑1 signature in human granulocytic MDSCs

biomarkers.name: exhausted CD8 T cells (PD‑1 high)
biomarkers.finding: high in pretreatment tumors; reduced after tadalafil + FLOT

models.name: Helicobacter felis infection Glee1‑LacZ reporter
models.genotype_or_key_features: wild‑type vs Glee1 knockout; metaplasia at 6 months

models.name: IL1B‑Tet‑On gastrin‑CreERT2 + MNU carcinogen
models.genotype_or_key_features: IL1B overexpression + nitrate carcinogen; tumor penetrance 45‑90%

models.name: Stage I‑III gastric cancer neoadjuvant window trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: 14 patients receiving tadalafil 20 mg daily for 2 weeks before FLOT

MOA_summary: Helicobacter infection induces type I interferon production by plasmacytoid dendritic cells, which together with Hedgehog signaling up‑regulates SLFN4 in granulocytic neutrophils, converting them into suppressive MDSCs that consume L‑arginine via arginase‑1 and maintain low cyclic GMP through PDE6 activity. Inhibition of PDE6 with tadalafil or sildenafil raises intracellular cGMP, activates PKG, and triggers apoptosis of these MDSCs, restoring CD8 T‑cell function and enhancing response to standard FLOT chemotherapy.
key_findings: Glee1 knockout mice retain normal gastric architecture despite Helicobacter infection, implicating Glee1‑positive myeloid cells in metaplasia.
SLFN4 expression marks granulocytic MDSCs; knockdown of SLFN4 increases apoptosis and reduces T‑cell suppression in vitro.
IL1B overexpression plus MNU yields gastric tumors in 45‑90% of mice; sildenafil treatment reduces tumor size and SLFN4‑positive cell infiltration.
In a human neoadjuvant window trial (n≈14), 2 weeks of tadalafil 20 mg daily decreased SLFN12L‑positive granulocytic MDSC frequency in tumor biopsies.
Subsequent FLOT chemotherapy after tadalafil further eliminated MDSCs and exhausted T‑cells while expanding cytotoxic CD8 T‑cells.
Bulk RNA‑seq of sorted SLFN4‑positive cells identified strong up‑regulation of PDE6, providing a mechanistic link to cyclic GMP metabolism.

combination_strategies.rationale: Standard neoadjuvant regimen; MDSC depletion by tadalafil may enhance chemotherapy‑induced tumor cell kill and immune activation.
combination_strategies.phenotype: complete loss of granulocytic MDSCs and exhausted T cells; increase in CD8 cytotoxic T cells in post‑treatment biopsies

open_questions: Can cyclic GMP phosphodiesterase inhibition prevent progression from intestinal metaplasia to invasive gastric cancer in a true preventive setting?
What is the relative contribution of PDE5 versus PDE6 inhibition to MDSC apoptosis, and can selective PDE6 inhibitors improve therapeutic index?
Does SLFN4 expression in T cells have functional relevance, and how does it affect overall immune remodeling?
